_meta.scored_by: Claude Opus 4.7 (same session as query author; ground truth taken from queries.json expected_intent)

scoring_scheme.match: top-1 satisfies expected_intent (generic canonical food, correct category and form)
scoring_scheme.partial: top-1 is in the right food category but wrong specific variant/preparation/brand context; not actively misleading for macros but would misrepresent micros or accuracy
scoring_scheme.miss: top-1 is in a different food category from the user's intent — using it would actively misrepresent the meal

scores.note: frozen mixture is scrambled eggs but a specific processed form; rank-2 'Egg, whole, cooked, scrambled' is the ideal canonical — ranking fails to prefer it

scores.top1: Babyfood, cereal, oatmeal, with honey, dry
scores.note: DB has 'Cereals, oats, regular and quick, prepared with water' but scorer surfaces baby food first; a user eating oatmeal for breakfast is NOT eating baby food
flavored branded entry beats generic plain — intent was plain greek yogurt; rank-3 is the canonical plain nonfat
user said brand, top-1 is that brand — variant choice is arbitrary when user didn't specify

deli roll product beats canonical 'Chicken, broilers or fryers, breast, skinless, boneless, meat only, cooked'; exact head-noun bonus fires for 'Chicken breast' (compound head) over 'Chicken' (single-token head)

scores.top1: Fast Foods, grilled chicken filet sandwich, with lettuce, tomato and spread
scores.note: fast-food sandwich wins; canonical 'Chicken, broiler, breast, grilled' is rank 4
portion wording stripped correctly; rice wins head-match; but 'glutinous uncooked' is an unusual choice when 'medium-grain enriched cooked' exists at rank 5

scores.top1: Snacks, rice cakes, brown rice, sesame seed
scores.note: tied score with real 'Rice, brown, long-grain, cooked' at 4.0; USDA naming 'Rice, brown' vs user 'brown rice' breaks the contiguous-phrase bonus; tie breaks to earlier CSV row which happens to be the snack

scores.query: two slices of whole wheat toast
scores.note: DB has 'Bread, whole-wheat, commercially prepared, toasted' but user says 'toast' which doesn't alias to 'bread' in our tokenizer; flour wins
'sandwich' in USDA naming refers to cookie sandwiches, not meal sandwiches; user's meaning of 'sandwich' is bread-based
clean head-noun match; plural-'s' normalization earns its keep here
raw apple, any variety — intent satisfied; nutrition-wise 'with skin' would be slightly better default

scores.top1: Beverages, SLIMFAST, Meal replacement, High Protein Shake, Ready-To-Drink, 3-2-1 plan
scores.note: branded meal-replacement RTD beats generic 'nutritional shake mix, high protein, powder' at rank 3; intent was whey/casein powder-based shake

scores.top1: Beverages, coffee, ready to drink, vanilla, light, milk based, sweetened
scores.note: RTD sweetened vanilla product doesn't represent brewed coffee + added milk; should decompose into two retrievals

scores.top1: Salad dressing, blue or roquefort cheese dressing, light
scores.note: 'salad' token dominates, matching dressings; spinach is not in top-5 at all — 'Spinach, raw' exists but scorer doesn't reach it
'fillet' as standalone product beats 'Fish, salmon, Atlantic...' which exists but has head 'Fish'; 'fillet' appears more centrally in 'Vegetarian fillets'
sea cucumber (animal) matches 'cucumber' token; composite meal would need pre-segmentation into {chicken, tortilla, cucumber} before retrieval
ties at 4.0 with 'Nuts, almonds' entries; CSV order puts granola bars first; scorer lacks a 'prefer less-processed' tiebreak
partial hit on avocado component; composite intent satisfied per queries.json ground truth
head-noun exact match, correct
